perApid.role: Spacecraft time packets (no PUS secondary header) — the session clock reference
perApid.decoded: OBT span 227049683.98 .. 227051882.50 s (~37 min). If CUC epoch = TGO launch 2016-03-14, session lands ~2023-05-24/25 = the ASIS transmission date (plausible, not proven).

perApid.role: TC verification acks for the memory-dump command stream to TC APID 0x01a — the metronome of the whole session
perApid.decoded: 1801 TCs, every one with a FULL success triplet (1,1)+(1,3)+(1,7); zero failures. Seqs = [476] + one contiguous block 7147..8946. Block: EXACT 1.000 s cadence (1799/1799 deltas), accept t / start t+0.200 s / complete t+0.200 s (start->complete = 0.0000 for all 1800); each TM(6,6) dump on 0017 emitted at the TC complete time. Seq 476 = setup command, all three acks at the IDENTICAL OBT 227049983.200, 0.3 s before the first block TC — chronologically first uplink ack of the session; TC(6,5) dump-memory reading suggested by event tail 01 06 05 (interpretation).

perApid.role: Housekeeping: parameter-management tables + boolean status report
perApid.decoded: 0x81 = parameter enable table: 313 used entries, 292 flagged 0x80 (enabled), 68 clear. 0x06 live fields: counter 40220->43820 (~1.87/s), byte@40 71->72, byte@42 down-ramp 148->114. Headline field: sid 0x06 f32@44 sits ~35197-35220 then jumps EXACTLY x4.000 to 140768 between OBT ~227051092 and 227051212 and stays.

perApid.role: Event reports bracketing every memory dump
perApid.decoded: Exactly TWO payload variants, 1800 each: phase=01 (start) and phase=00 (complete). Merged 0016/0017 stream is a perfect (event01, dump, event00) x1800 pattern — regex fullmatch; all three packets of each triple share the SAME OBT; triples 1.000 s apart (1799/1799). Tail 06 05 = PUS service 6 subservice 5 (dump memory) — execution start/completion events of each TC(6,5).

perApid.role: THE hidden ASIS message — memory dump reports (already fully decoded by community; verified here byte-for-byte)
perApid.decoded: Addresses strictly contiguous 0..8212 (addr[i]+len[i]==addr[i+1] for all 1799 pairs); reassembled 8212 B == binaire.bin exactly. Each dump emitted at its TC's complete time (accept+0.2 s), 1 Hz.

perApid.role: Fast diagnostics of one unit with an internal ~160 s duty cycle — NOT correlated with the dump activity
perApid.decoded: u32@10 free-runs at ~16379 Hz (nominal 2^14) and loses ~64500 ticks (~3.94 s) every ~160 s — 13 events, flag@116 drops 1->0 one packet after 12/13 of them; the cycle exists BEFORE the dump window, so it is an internal duty cycle of the unit. hk_fields' 'f32@19' is a misparse: region is u16 channels (u16@18 in {202,203}; @26 = noisy ADC 3981-5046). Dumps-per-8s-interval is 8-or-0 with the stream unchanged across the window boundary (u16@26 mean 4545 in-window vs 4582 out).

perApid.role: Housekeeping carrying the ONLY parameter found that tracks the dump window — a x4/x16 rate/load register
perApid.decoded: f32@287 takes only 3 values = 132100 x {1,4,16}: 132100 before the dumps, x16 (2113600) at OBT 227050051 (~67 s after dump start 227049983.7), dips to x4 (528400) at 227050411/227050891/227051731, back to 132100 at 227051851 (~68 s after dump end 227051782.7). Reads like a data-rate/load setting (132100 ~ 132.1 kbps?) — the best HK correlate of the dump session.

perApid.role: Minimal subsystem heartbeat — fully decoded uptime report
perApid.decoded: Uptime 20772 -> 24860 s at rate 65535.87/s => subsystem boot epoch OBT ~227028990.8 (~5.8 h before session). One delta short by 256 ticks (-3.9 ms) at interval 12. 16/17 deltas = exactly 120*65536.

perApid.role: Software/CPU statistics of a processing unit (coarse — only 4 samples)
perApid.decoded: Task/CPU tick and load counters; f32 ranges e.g. @31 -4413..-0.0014 (mono incr), @392 3.03..24.91 (incr), @204 2.19e8..7.78e8. Caveat: several huge-magnitude 'f32' fields (@78 5.65e9, @194 4.62e9) may be misclassified counter bytes (only 4 samples at 600 s).

perApid.role: Thermal + device HK hub: the only real ASCII in the session (equipment ID tables), the fastest HK (8 s), a rotating mechanism, and bulk thermistor tables
perApid.decoded: (1) ID tables: '466_CAL_06 / NJ7_BIT_00 / NE4_CTL_... / 3R4_DGN_00', serials '3000_SN460/552/553', '9666NJ_466 001_454768 002_454935 003_455241' (unit 466) and the 467 twin — two redundant units reporting CAL/BIT/CTL/DGN versions + serial numbers, literal placeholder 'xxxxxxxxxx' for one unpopulated SN. (2) sid 0x2a offset 29 'mode string' CTOF(x159)/CUOF(x39)/CUPF(x81)/EWRF(x1) is NOT text: three u8 channels drifting +1-2 counts beside a f32 exponent byte 0x46; the lone EWRF = simultaneous +2/+3 spike for exactly one 8-s sample at OBT 227050671.9, ~51 s before the two 0x2fa TCs. (3) 0x69 = continuously rotating mechanism: counters advance 40013/120 s mod 65536 => 196.5 s period (0.305 rpm) uninterrupted all session; 14 valid thermistors 2.85-15.0 C, all warming. (4) 0x1d: eng 876.7 -> peak 991.1 (samples 26-28) -> 984.0 with rawA MSB inverse mirror; rawB ALSO peaks (33610->38714@21->29603), not a counter. (5) 0x25: 7-channel lockstep decay ~35-40% (e.g. 1075->688) — charge/current tail-off-like; u16@62 = exact +300/30 s (10 Hz). (6) NTC calibration shared with 00f3 (beta 3523 K); hot triplet 38.2-40.7 C in 0x28 matches 00f3 hot group.
perApid.conf: proven (ASCII, CTOF artifact, rotation, tick, tables); device identities open

perApid.role: On-board monitoring (FDIR) configuration dump: one limit-check definition table + one group/membership table, windowed across SIDs
perApid.decoded: A PUS parameter-monitoring/limit-check table (~185 populated checks; multi-range checks: param 0x5a022fd appears 8x with different limits) plus a monitoring-group membership table (first group: 10 members [1,2,3,4,0x2a-0x2d,0x3d,0xa6]; member ID range matches 0013/0x81 param IDs). Check-ID continuity across SIDs proves they are memory windows, not independent reports. Only live bit in the whole APID: sid 0xae payload byte 249 alternating 0x30/0x38 per report.

perApid.role: TC verification acks for auxiliary device commands to TC APID 0x0ba
perApid.decoded: Seqs contiguous 2445..2468, zero failures. Arrive as 8 bursts of 3 (intra-burst deltas 1.0 s then 2.5 s), ALL during the dump train (after dumps #36/#95/#678/#756/#1056/#1164/#1721/#1786); bursts pair into 4 two-phase operations (intra-pair gap 55.9-104.2 s, inter-pair 296.5-578.8 s) — repeated reconfig interleaved with the dump (target device unidentified).

perApid.role: AOCS/attitude housekeeping — the physics-richest APID: quaternions, rates, Sun/Earth vectors, alignment matrices
perApid.decoded: Attitude: |q|^2 in [0.99999993,1.00000006] for all 561 pkts; spacecraft rotated 148.10 deg over 2240 s, |omega| 0.85e-3..1.49e-3 rad/s mean 1.15e-3 (0.066 deg/s) ~ TGO 2-h orbital rate => slow steered pointing, not inertial hold. Rates verified kinematically: omega = 2*conj(q)*qdot matches @42/46/50 with RMS residual [4.0e-5,1.6e-5,2.4e-5] rad/s vs signal ~1.2e-3. Pointing control: error quaternion = identity within 28.6 arcsec (fine 3-axis wheel control). Sun vector (const inertial [0.894,-0.397,-0.206], drift <0.012 deg) proven geometrically via the solar-array angle formula (max diff 7.2e-4 rad, f20==-f16 bit-exact 555/561); Earth vector [0.530,-0.767,-0.362] plausible (31.41 deg to Sun ~ Mars-view Sun-Earth separation on 2023-05-24) = HGA target. Alignment: nominal DCM with exact 22.5 deg cant vs calibrated 22.148 deg copy (star tracker/IMU mounting, strong). Unidentified: 4-channel group 27.4-65.3 (wheel speeds 260-620 rpm vs temps), deg channels @119/127/131, accelerating integrators 0 -> -0.87.
perApid.conf: proven (quats/rates/vector geometry/error quat/DCM orthonormality); Sun=strong, Earth=plausible. Correction from verification: the 'no other quaternion exists' exhaustiveness claim was refuted — sids 0x3d/0x3e@381/0x3f/0x34 hold additional (consistent) unit structures

perApid.role: HK of a device pair (or differential measurement) with counter-phased slow ramps
perApid.decoded: 0x2f f32@9 falls +1.89 -> -0.91 while 0x9f f32@8 rises -1.93 -> +1.16 — near-opposite ramps, but both NOISY (step swings 0.5-1.0): paired sums reach |0.905|, so they are NOT a tight complementary pair (verification correction); counter-phased trend only. The recurring 'Aev?rr??' ASCII = the const 14.3413 f32 bytes.

perApid.role: HK of a 2-axis articulated mechanism (gimbal-like): two angles with redundant A/B channels
perApid.decoded: theta1 = 0.785 rad (45.0 deg) -> min 0.284 (16.3 deg) mid-session -> 1.143 (65.5 deg), V-shaped; theta2 = +0.170 rad (9.7 deg) -> min -0.627 at sample 29 -> ends -0.521 (-29.8 deg) — mean rate -3.2e-4 rad/s (-1.10 deg/min) but NOT monotonic (rises over last 7 samples; correction). Rate does not match the 0053/0x69 rotation; gimbal vs sun-aspect identity open.

perApid.role: Near-static HK of a cold unit: two thermistors + zeroed event/counter tables
perApid.decoded: Only 8 varying byte positions of 204. Temps: 5.45-6.35 C (non-monotonic) and 2.72-3.08 C (warming). Both counters zero all session — the monitored events never occurred.

perApid.role: 101-channel thermistor acquisition system with on-board NTC conversion
perApid.decoded: Raw/eng perfectly anticorrelated per channel (65 channels at r<-0.9999, worst -0.9988); ONE deterministic calibration curve (1919 pooled pairs, 502 unique raws, 0 conflicts; NTC beta fit 3523 K; invalid marker raw 11507200 -> -89.816 C). 5 channels OFF (9,19,38,64,90; no toggles): ch19/64 open-circuit, ch90 frozen at 1.18 C, ch38 genuinely cold external (-19.0..-12.95 C). Census of means: 55 ch 15-25 C, 23 ch 5-15 C, 8 ch 0-5 C, 7 <0 C, 1 25-35 C, 7 hot 36.3-41.5 C (near dissipating units). Trend correction: NOT uniformly warming — of 93 varying channels 48 warm / 45 cool (extremes ch38 +6.07 C, ch94 -2.73 C).

perApid.role: TC verification acks for 7 device-access commands to TC APID 0x15a
perApid.decoded: Two clusters during dumps #913-#1138 (accepts 227050896.1/898.1/899.1 and 227051073.1/1078.1/1106.1/1121.1). Exact 1:1 with the 7 TM(2,130) on 015b: each report at TC accept+0.200 s = the TC COMPLETE time (the (1,3) start ack shares the accept OBT — verification correction: report coincides with complete, not start).

perApid.role: Software HK of a processing unit: seven synchronized uptime clocks + filter-like floats
perApid.decoded: Local uptime ~32982.7 s at session start => THIS unit's boot epoch OBT ~227016705 — a DIFFERENT boot time than 0033's (~227028990.8): at least two independent power-on events ~3.4 h apart before the session.

perApid.role: Device access reports answering the 7 TCs to 0x15a — a single-register readout
perApid.decoded: Data byte sequence in time order: 0x40, 0x50, 0x4d, 0x4d, 0x54, 0x4d, 0x4d = ASCII '@','P','M','M','T','M','M' (0x4d x4). Reads like polling one status register: two state changes then 'M' (nominal/ready?) confirmed 4x — meaning open.

perApid.role: Software HK of another unit: clocks + one strongly diverging float
perApid.decoded: f32@20 monotone DECREASING -1.21e6 -> -2.06e11 (magnitude x1.7e5 over the session — an accumulating/diverging quantity); f32@35 starts 33049, ends 129.09.

perApid.role: Software HK pair: one near-dead stream + one counter-rich stream with sporadic event snapshots
perApid.decoded: The 559240/s triple = three identical fast task/interrupt counters; @196-@216 occasionally latch nonzero values (e.g. @202 in {00,82,84,87}) = sporadic event snapshot area.

perApid.role: Software HK: duplicated slow counter + 256 kHz counter
perApid.decoded: Clean counter block; 256000/s = 256 kHz tick. f32@45 -1.03e10..-6.41e8 (large negative accumulator).

perApid.role: TC verification acks for the 2 anomalous commands to TC APID 0x2fa — a different ack mask and off-grid timing
perApid.pus: TM(1,1)/(1,7) ONLY (no (1,3) start ack — unique in the session)
perApid.decoded: TC1 accept 227050722.9708, complete +0.53 ms; TC2 accept 227050727.9681, complete +55.5 ms; accepts 5.00 s apart, sent after dump #740. Genuinely anomalous: (i) only accept+complete acks (different requested ack mask), (ii) OFF the 0.1-s scheduler grid — fine fractions .9708/.9681/.0237 vs all other 5475 acks on {.0,.1,...,.8} — suggesting a different command source/route. CORRECTION (verification): the earlier destID-0x02 'anomaly' is FALSE — 0x02 is the majority convention (00b2 72/72, 0152 21/21, 0012 seq476 3/3); only the 0012 dump-block acks use 0x04.

perApid.role: Near-constant status page of the 0x2fa-side unit with ONE live mode byte
perApid.decoded: byte@591: value 3 (25 pkts) vs 2 (12 pkts); windows at 2: [227050979,227051039), [227051159,227051699), [227051759,227051879). First 3->2 onset is 236-256 s AFTER the 0x2fa TCs (227050722.97/727.97) but within one 60-s sample of the 0053/0x2a mode change CTOF->CUOF at 227050943.9 — the mode-change link is the better fit; direct TC link weak.

perApid.role: Idle/fill APID — pure pseudo-random filler, NO hidden content
perApid.decoded: Entropy 7.9991 bits/byte yet zlib-compresses to 2.98% (structured); classic m-sequence 0x00 suppression (3527 vs mean 7163); 224,049/229,230 words are exact 64-bit PN9 matches, 229,230/229,230 at valid phase; no FFFF0690 sync marker, no 0FFF pair, no headers, no payload. Open oddity: why the stride is exactly +113 (=64+49) bits.

headline: Full uplink reconstruction (community #TODO), verified: 1834 TCs across 4 TC APIDs (1801 to 0x01a, 24 to 0x0ba, 7 to 0x15a, 2 to 0x2fa) with ZERO failure acks; the 1800-dump train is a 1.000-s metronome with deterministic +0.200-s execution and a perfect event-dump-event triple at identical OBTs; setup TC seq 476 triple-acked 0.3 s before the train; the 2 anomalous 0x2fa TCs use a reduced ack mask (accept+complete) and off-grid timestamps.

AOCS reconstructed rigorously (community only had raw hex): scalar-first attitude quaternion (unit norm to 3.6e-8, 561 samples) + body rates cross-checked kinematically (residual 3.8e-5 rad/s). Verified result: a 148-degree-PATH MULTI-axis re-orientation (131.7 deg net, rotation-axis wobble 25 deg) at 0.066 deg/s - NOT an inertial hold, no arcsec pointing lock and no Earth-track (body-axis inertial wobble 35-71 deg).

Community open question tested: the short (4B) vs long (8B) dump order is NOT a proven hidden channel - the 8-byte reads land on richer memory (per-byte density 1.26x: 0.098 vs 0.078 set-bits/byte; 67% vs 85% all-zero), consistent with local data density but only weakly (correlation r~0.17).

Reconstructed the dumped memory map (memory ID 0x06): the message sits at base address ZERO, region 0x0000-0x2014 (8212 B), dumped as a strictly linear address sweep with NOTHING beyond it (no gaps/overlaps/margin bytes). Every read is 4-byte aligned and the 8-byte reads are two 32-bit words (49% at addr%8=4) => 32-bit memory; the 2-byte trailer is the ECSS ISO-16 checksum over the data, validating 100% (community had ~95%).

A complete 101-channel thermistor system decoded (00f3): three blocked arrays (enable flags, raw NTC ohms, converted degC), one session-derived NTC calibration (beta ~3547 K, 0 conflicts over 1919 pairs, 7 failed channels excluded), a commutation index decrementing exactly -12 mod 101, and a 7-channel hot group at 36.3-41.5 C.

On-board FDIR configuration reconstructed (0063): seven HK SIDs proven to be windows of ONE contiguous 200x37-B limit-check table (check-IDs 0x18120004-0x181202ef strictly increasing across SID boundaries) plus a 100x126-B group-membership table (records align within each report; the logical table spans reports) - parameter-monitoring config, not telemetry.

The idle stream (07ff, 1.83 MB) is dominated by PN9 (x^9+x^5+1): 97.74% (224,049/229,230) of 8-byte words are exact PN9 phase windows (5,181 fail every m-sequence convention => not pure PN9), a +113-bit phase stride, no FFFF0690/0FFF markers, no headers - closing the 'is there more in the fill data' question.

The only HK parameter that tracks the message-dump window: 0023/0x13 f32@287 steps exactly x16 (132100 -> 2113600) 67 s after the train starts and reverts 68 s after it ends; plus a decoded 1:1 command-response chain 0x15a TC -> TM(2,130) register readout '@PMMTMM' on 015b.

Timeline anchors: OBT span 227,049,676-227,051,917 (37.4 min) maps to 2023-05-24 ~21:21-21:59 UTC if the CUC epoch is TGO launch (matches the ASIS transmission date); two distinct subsystem boot epochs recovered from uptime clocks (0033 ~OBT 227,028,991; 0153 ~227,016,706, ~3.4 h earlier).
